title: lobelia puberula subsp. pauciflora
intro: Lobelia puberula subsp. pauciflora, also known as the "few-flowered lobelia," is a plant species in the Lobeliaceae family, characterized by its delicate purple flowers and hairy leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been employed to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Lobelia puberula subsp. pauciflora are attributed to its ability to soothe and calm the respiratory system, reduce inflammation, and promote relaxation. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve infusions or tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, or capsules for medicinal purposes.
